topic_coverage.quoted_user_text: I miss categories all over the toolings — filter categories in nearly all features and charts; see which category performs badly or well; per-category expense trends (Jan 300€, Feb 250€, March 400€) with a clear chart; use categories in planning and forecast what-ifs like spending less in crypto.

Self-hosted Flow Finance AI on omniflow external profile with Firefly III mirror as transaction source; operator already syncs 900+ transactions; features must work in React SPA and embedded Grafana analytics routes.
Extend existing Rust Axum backend, React TypeScript SPA, TimescaleDB mirror, Grafana provisioning — no new runtime; category data from Firefly sync mirror columns post-BUG-0006.

Read-only toward Firefly; new category filters and subscription tags live in product DB and APIs; reuse US-0015 bucket mapping and US-0006 AI tools where planning suggestions need inference; vertical story split not one mega-epic.

Category filter controls where charts and tables allow; line or bar chart for monthly category spend; per-plan statistics pages separate from household totals; subscription search by account title and repeating months; custom tags like luxus and important on subscriptions.
No Firefly write-back for tags or category overrides; AI savings suggestions under allow_raw_transactions=false aggregate signals; operator confirms subscription candidates manually.
Prefer incremental delivery in three stories; Grafana and React parity for category analytics; performance acceptable on 12–24 month category series; OIDC external profile regression pass.

Three user stories: US-0018 category filters and trend analytics; US-0019 goal-driven planning with per-plan stats and AI savings; US-0020 subscription manual discovery majority-category and operator tags; discovery then architecture before execute.

plan_area_inventory.title: Category filters and expense trend analytics
Goal-driven planning with per-plan statistics and AI savings
Subscription manual discovery, majority category, operator tags

decomposition.rationale: Operator requested three independent capability clusters (categories/analytics, planning/goals, subscriptions/tags) with distinct surfaces and acceptance — vertical slices preserve independent delivery value.
decomposition.split_axis: user-value workflow (analytics vs planning vs subscriptions)

boundaries.US-0018: Cross-feature category filter contract + per-category monthly expense trends/charts — foundation for category what-ifs elsewhere
boundaries.US-0019: Per-plan statistics, target-balance-by-date plans, category-scoped adjustments, AI savings suggestions — extends US-0004/US-0014
boundaries.US-0020: Manual subscription search/confirm, majority-category assignment, operator-defined tags — extends US-0003 detection

alternatives_considered: Single mega-epic US-0018 — rejected (cross-cutting but three delivery surfaces; operator explicitly asked for more than one US)
Four stories (split category filter from trend charts) — rejected (filter + chart share same API/mirror contract; keep one analytics story)
Defer AI savings to US-0006 only — rejected (operator wants selectable suggestions when building goal plans)

dependencies.US-0019: Benefits from US-0018 category filter APIs for what-if lines; can ship with minimal category picker if US-0018 lags
dependencies.US-0020: Independent of US-0018; uses existing subscription mirror
